question: What are your working hours?
answer: We are open Monday to Saturday, 9 AM to 7 PM. We are closed on Sundays and public holidays. For emergencies, please call our helpline.

question: Are you open on Sundays?
answer: We are closed on Sundays. Our working hours are Monday to Saturday, 9 AM to 7 PM. We recommend booking appointments in advance to avoid waiting.

question: Which doctors are available?
answer: We have Dr. Priya Nair (General Physician), Dr. Arjun Mehta (Cardiologist), Dr. Sunita Rao (Gynaecologist), and Dr. Ramesh Kumar (Orthopaedic). Availability varies by day.

question: How do I book an appointment?
answer: You can book an appointment right here by telling me your name, preferred date and time, and the doctor you wish to see. I will confirm your slot immediately.

question: Can I reschedule my appointment?
answer: Yes, you can reschedule your appointment. Please contact us at least 2 hours before your scheduled time. Just tell me your name and preferred new slot and I will update it.

question: What are the consultation fees?
answer: General consultation is ₹400 for new patients and ₹300 for follow-up visits. Specialist consultations range from ₹600 to ₹1200 depending on the doctor.

question: Do you accept health insurance?
answer: Yes, we accept most major health insurance policies including Star Health, HDFC Ergo, and Mediclaim. Please carry your insurance card and ID proof when visiting.

question: Where are you located?
answer: We are located at 42, MG Road, Bengaluru — near the Metro Station, behind HDFC Bank. We have ample parking available. Google Maps link available on request.

question: How long is the waiting time?
answer: With a prior appointment, waiting time is usually 10-15 minutes. Walk-in patients may wait 30-60 minutes depending on the day. We recommend booking in advance.

question: Is there an emergency facility?
answer: We have a first-aid and basic emergency facility. For serious emergencies, we will stabilise you and refer to the nearest hospital. Our emergency number is 080-12345678.

question: What documents should I bring for my first visit?
answer: For your first visit, please bring a valid photo ID (Aadhaar/PAN), your insurance card if applicable, and any previous medical reports or prescriptions.

question: Do you offer online consultations?
answer: Yes, we offer video consultations for follow-up visits and non-emergency queries. Online consultation fees are ₹250. Book the same way as an in-person appointment.

question: What services does the clinic offer?
answer: We offer general medicine, cardiology, gynaecology, orthopaedics, pathology lab (blood tests), ECG, X-ray, and pharmacy. All under one roof for your convenience.

question: How do I cancel an appointment?
answer: To cancel, please inform us at least 2 hours in advance so we can offer your slot to another patient. Just message me your name and appointment time to cancel.

question: Is there a pharmacy at the clinic?
answer: Yes, we have an in-house pharmacy stocked with common medicines. Our pharmacy is open 9 AM to 8 PM, Monday to Saturday. We also accept prescriptions from outside.

question: Do you have facilities for children?
answer: Yes, we have a paediatric OPD every Tuesday and Thursday with Dr. Kavita Nambiar. We also have a child-friendly waiting area with toys and books.

question: What are the blood test facilities available?
answer: Our in-house pathology lab offers complete blood count, sugar levels, lipid profile, thyroid tests, urine tests, and more. Reports are available within 4-6 hours for most tests.

question: Is parking available?
answer: Yes, we have free parking for up to 50 cars and secure 2-wheeler parking in our basement. Parking is free for the first 2 hours for clinic patients.

question: Do you provide home visit services?
answer: Yes, we offer home visits for elderly patients and post-surgery care within a 5 km radius. Home visit charges are ₹800 per visit. Please call to schedule.

question: What are the payment methods accepted?
answer: We accept cash, UPI (PhonePe, GPay, Paytm), all major credit and debit cards, and net banking. We do not accept cheques.

question: How do I get my reports?
answer: Lab reports can be collected from our reception or sent to you via WhatsApp or email. Please share your contact details when collecting samples.

question: Do you have a senior citizen discount?
answer: Yes, we offer 15% discount on consultation fees for senior citizens (60 years and above). Please carry age proof at the time of visit.

question: Is there a waiting area with air conditioning?
answer: Yes, our clinic has a fully air-conditioned waiting area with comfortable seating, water dispenser, and TV. We also have a separate waiting area for contagious patients.

question: Can I speak to a doctor on the phone?
answer: For quick queries, our doctors offer 10-minute phone consultations for ₹150. For detailed consultations, we recommend an in-person or video appointment.

question: What should I do in case of a medical emergency?
answer: For life-threatening emergencies, call 108 (national ambulance) immediately. Our clinic first-aid number is 080-12345678. Do not drive yourself — call for help first.
